content_id:
Syarh al-Sunnah telah mempertahankan posisinya dalam keilmuan dan pendidikan Islam selama sembilan abad, sebuah kesaksian atas kualitas dan kemanfaatannya. Kombinasi teks-teks hadis yang andal dengan komentar yang mudah diakses menjadikannya berharga sekaligus sebagai teks pengajaran maupun sebagai referensi bagi para ulama yang bekerja pada topik-topik yang berkaitan.

Di dunia Syafi'i, Syarh al-Sunnah khususnya banyak diajarkan, karena pembentukannya dalam tradisi Syafi'i menjadikan komentarnya sangat relevan bagi para pelajar yang belajar dalam tradisi tersebut. Namun, penyajian yang seimbang atas posisi-posisi mazhab lain mencegah karya ini menjadi teks yang hanya untuk satu mazhab tertentu, dan ulama-ulama Hanafi dan Hanbali pun menemukan nilai di dalamnya.

Masabih al-Sunnah karya al-Baghawi, kumpulan hadis tematik yang berkaitan tanpa komentar panjang, beredar bahkan lebih luas dari Syarh al-Sunnah sendiri, menjangkau para pelajar melalui versi yang diperluas yang diproduksi oleh al-Khatib al-Tibrizi (Misykat al-Mashabih). Karya turunan ini membawa seleksi hadis al-Baghawi yang diambil dari Syarh al-Sunnah kepada khalayak global dari para pelajar madrasah yang mungkin tidak mempelajari komentar lengkapnya.

Bagi para pelajar masa kini, Syarh al-Sunnah menawarkan beberapa keistimewaan sebagai teks bacaan. Kombinasi teks hadis dan komentar praktis dalam satu karya berarti bahwa seorang pelajar yang membacanya mengembangkan keakraban sekaligus dengan korpus hadis dan tradisi penafsirannya secara bersamaan. Aksesibilitas prosa Arab al-Baghawi menjadikannya lebih mudah dibaca dibanding beberapa komentar hadis yang lebih teknis.

Edisi kritis modern Syarh al-Sunnah yang diterbitkan oleh al-Maktab al-Islami (Beirut dan Damaskus, 1983) terdiri dari enam belas jilid dan menyajikan teks yang dapat diandalkan dengan aparatus ilmiah dasar. Membaca karya ini dalam edisi tersebut, dengan konsultasi tambahan terhadap referensi hadis dan rijal yang lebih khusus untuk pertanyaan-pertanyaan tertentu, merupakan pendekatan yang sangat baik untuk pembelajaran serius tentang hadis dan hubungannya dengan praktik Islam. Para pelajar yang menyelesaikan bacaan terstruktur Syarh al-Sunnah akan keluar dengan penguasaan yang kuat atas riwayat-riwayat kenabian utama dalam konteks hukum dan etisnya, serta apresiasi terhadap cara tradisi komentar klasik mengintegrasikan kritik tekstual, penalaran hukum, dan bimbingan praktis ke dalam satu penyajian terpadu dari warisan kenabian.
content_en:
Sharh as-Sunnah has maintained a place in Islamic scholarship and education across nine centuries, a testament to its quality and utility. The work's combination of reliable hadith texts with accessible commentary made it valuable both as a teaching text and as a reference for scholars working on related topics.

In the Shafi'i world, Sharh as-Sunnah was particularly widely taught, as al-Baghawi's Shafi'i formation made his commentary especially relevant for students learning within that tradition. However, the work's balanced presentation of other schools' positions prevented it from becoming exclusively a school-specific text, and Hanafi and Hanbali scholars also found value in it.

Al-Baghawi's Masabih as-Sunnah, his related thematic hadith selection without extended commentary, became even more widely circulated than Sharh as-Sunnah itself, reaching students through the expanded version produced by al-Khatib at-Tibrizi (Mishkat al-Masabih). This derivative work brought al-Baghawi's selection of hadiths — drawn from Sharh as-Sunnah — to a global audience of madrasa students who might not have studied the full commentary.

For contemporary students, Sharh as-Sunnah offers several distinctive benefits as a reading text. Its combination of hadith text and practical commentary in a single work means that a student reading it develops familiarity with both the hadith corpus and the interpretive tradition simultaneously. The accessibility of al-Baghawi's Arabic prose makes it more readable than some of the more technical hadith commentaries.

The modern critical edition of Sharh as-Sunnah, published by al-Maktab al-Islami (Beirut and Damascus, 1983), runs to sixteen volumes and provides a reliable text with basic scholarly apparatus. Reading the work in this edition, with supplementary consultation of the more specialized hadith and rijal references for specific questions, represents an excellent approach to the serious study of hadith and its relationship to Islamic practice. Students who complete a structured reading of Sharh as-Sunnah will emerge with a solid command of the major prophetic narrations in their legal and ethical context, as well as an appreciation for the classical commentary tradition's way of integrating textual criticism, legal reasoning, and practical guidance into a unified presentation of the prophetic legacy.
content_ar: Sharh as-السنة has maintained a place in Islamic scholarship and education across nine centuries, a testament to its quality and utility. The work's combination of reliable الحديث texts with accessible commentary made it valuable both as a teaching text and as a reference for العلماء working on related topics. In the الشافعي الدنيا, Sharh as-السنة was particularly widely taught, as al-Baghawi's الشافعي formation made his commentary ولا سيما relevant for students learning within that التراث. غير أن, the work's balanced presentation of other schools' positions prevented it from becoming exclusively a school-specific text, and الحنفي and الحنبلي العلماء أيضاً found value in it. Al-Baghawi's Masabih as-السنة, his related thematic الحديث selection without extended commentary, became even more widely circulated than Sharh as-السنة itself, reaching students through the expanded version produced by al-Khatib at-Tibrizi (Mishkat al-Masabih). This derivative work brought al-Baghawi's selection of hadiths — drawn from Sharh as-السنة — to a global audience of madrasa students who might not have studied the full commentary. For contemporary students, Sharh as-السنة offers several distinctive benefits as a reading text. Its combination of الحديث text and practical commentary in a single work means that a student reading it develops familiarity with both the الحديث corpus and the interpretive التراث simultaneously. The accessibility of al-Baghawi's Arabic prose makes it more readable than some of the more technical الحديث commentaries. The modern البالغ الأهمية edition of Sharh as-السنة, published by al-Maktab al-Islami (Beirut and Damascus, 1983), runs to sixteen volumes and provides a reliable text with الأساسي العلمي apparatus. Reading the work in this edition, with supplementary consultation of the more specialized الحديث and rijal references for specific questions, represents an excellent approach to the serious study of الحديث and its relationship to Islamic practice. Students who complete a structured reading of Sharh as-السنة will emerge with a solid command of the major prophetic narrations in their legal and ethical context, as well as an appreciation for the الكلاسيكي commentary التراث's الطريق of integrating textual criticism, legal reasoning, and practical الهداية into a unified presentation of the prophetic legacy.
